anchorSettlements.name: Ashford
anchorSettlements.description: Your home town, a quiet farming village at the foot of gentle hills. The King's Road begins here, stretching north toward legend.

anchorSettlements.name: Millbrook
anchorSettlements.description: A prosperous market town where the King's Road crosses the River Mill. Traders from across the realm gather here.

anchorSettlements.name: Thornfield Ruins
anchorSettlements.description: Ancient ruins perched on a hilltop, said to hold clues to the Grail's location. Pilgrims and fortune-seekers camp at the base.

anchorSettlements.name: Ravensgate
anchorSettlements.description: A walled city under the iron grip of Lord Ashwick. The King's Road passes through its heavily guarded gates.

anchorSettlements.name: The Pilgrim's Rest
anchorSettlements.description: A monastery clinging to a mountain pass, offering shelter to travellers brave enough to cross the highlands.

anchorSettlements.name: Grailsend
anchorSettlements.description: The final temple at the northern edge of the realm, where the Holy Grail awaits one worthy enough to claim it.

regions.name: Ashford Meadows
regions.description: Gentle rolling farmland stretching to the southern coast. Sheep graze on green hills divided by ancient stone walls.

regions.name: The Millwood
regions.description: Ancient woodland where the River Mill winds between towering oaks. Woodcutters and charcoal burners work the forest edges.

regions.name: Thornfield Hills
regions.description: Exposed hilltops scattered with the ruins of a civilization older than memory. The wind never stops here.

regions.name: The Bleakmoors
regions.description: A desolate moorland under perpetual grey skies. Lord Ashwick's patrols are the only law here.

regions.name: The Pilgrim's Way
regions.description: A narrow pass through the central mountains, kept clear by the monks of the Pilgrim's Rest.

regions.name: The Grailsend Highlands
regions.description: Forbidding peaks at the northern edge of the realm. Few return from these heights, but the Grail calls the worthy onward.

regions.name: The Saltmarsh Coast
regions.description: A windswept coastline of salt marshes and tidal flats. Fishing villages cling to the rocky shore.

regions.name: The Chalk Downs
regions.description: Gentle chalk downland on the eastern side of the realm. Rich farmland feeds the kingdom's towns.

regions.name: The Deepwood
regions.description: The oldest forest in the realm. Even the King's Road skirts its borders. Strange lights flicker between the trees at night.

regions.name: The Fenlands
regions.description: Treacherous marshland east of the moors. Home to outcasts, hermits, and things best left undisturbed.

offRoadSettlements.name: Whitecliff
offRoadSettlements.description: A small fishing port clinging to white chalk cliffs. Smugglers use the sea caves below.

offRoadSettlements.name: Woodhaven
offRoadSettlements.description: A tiny settlement of charcoal burners and woodcutters at the edge of the Deepwood.

offRoadSettlements.name: Barrowfield
offRoadSettlements.description: A farming hamlet surrounded by ancient barrow mounds. The locals leave offerings at dusk.

offRoadSettlements.name: Fenwatch
offRoadSettlements.description: A lonely watchtower garrisoned by soldiers nobody else wanted. They watch the marshes for movement.

offRoadSettlements.name: Saltmarsh
offRoadSettlements.description: A weatherbeaten village of salt farmers and net menders. They say a sea serpent lives in the bay.

offRoadSettlements.name: Cairnholm
offRoadSettlements.description: A windswept cluster of stone houses around a cairn said to mark a hero's grave.

offRoadSettlements.name: Eagle's Perch
offRoadSettlements.description: The last outpost before the Grailsend peaks. Rangers here guide the foolhardy onward.

offRoadSettlements.name: The Crossing
offRoadSettlements.description: A river crossing on a lesser road through the Millwood. A ferryman charges a coin per head.
